response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application-layer development. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency async I/O work at Dunnhumby and some embedded/robotics tooling at Altom, but these are several levels removed from the hardware architecture and silicon-level performance analysis the role requires.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency systems programming experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec)
Broad multi-domain engineering background including embedded systems, robotics tooling, and infrastructure — shows adaptability beyond pure web work
15+ years of professional experience satisfies the Bachelor's + 8yr educational path requirement on paper
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or any silicon/hardware architecture experience whatsoever
No AI, deep learning, or ML workload expertise — the core technical domain of the role
No GPU, SIMD, or MIMD background; the entire career is software-layer (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang web frameworks)
